name: cryptsetup-bin
title: cryptsetup-bin
summary: disk encryption support - command line tools
description:
disk encryption support - command line tools
Cryptsetup provides an interface for configuring encryption on block
devices (such as /home or swap partitions), using the Linux kernel
device mapper target dm-crypt. It features integrated Linux Unified Key
Setup (LUKS) support.
.
This package provides the cryptsetup, integritysetup and veritysetup
utilities.

commands.name: cryptsetup
commands.description: utility for configuring and managing encrypted storage devices

arguments.description: acrypt-query-pim to be prompted for the PIM.
arguments.raw: --ver  acrypt-query-pim to be prompted for the PIM.

commands.name: integritysetup
commands.description: utility for configuring and managing dm-integrity devices

commands.name: veritysetup
commands.description: utility for configuring and managing dm-verity devices

command_examples: Cryptsetup returns 0 on success and a non-zero value on error.
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use cryptsetup because disk encryption support - command line tools.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use cryptsetup as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `cryptsetup <action> [<options>] <action args>` as documented upstream for cryptsetup.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `sudo cryptsetup --type luks2 luksFormat /dev/sdX` as documented upstream for cryptsetup.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `sudo cryptsetup luksAddKey --key-slot 5 /dev/sdX` as documented upstream for cryptsetup.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `sudo cryptsetup luksHeaderBackup /dev/sdX --header-backup-file /var/tmp/NameOfBackupFile` as documented upstream for cryptsetup.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair cryptsetup with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `cryptsetup --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

arguments.name: --ver
arguments.description: acrypt-query-pim to be prompted for the PIM.

examples: Cryptsetup returns 0 on success and a non-zero value on error.
